How Clothing Startups Can Manufacture from 50 Pieces

July 1, 2026 | By FABRIOZA

Starting a clothing brand is one of the most exciting entrepreneurial journeys you can take. But there's a catch: most clothing manufacturers want you to order 300-500 pieces minimum before they'll even talk to you. For a startup with no sales history, that's a terrifying commitment. At FABRIOZA, we built our business around solving this exact problem — with MOQ starting at just 50 pieces and a startup-friendly process designed for new brands.

Why Startups Need Low MOQ Manufacturing

The average clothing startup fails because of inventory risk, not design quality. Ordering 500 pieces of an untested design ties up $3,000-8,000 in stock that might not sell. With 50 pieces, your investment drops to $400-900 — low enough to test, learn, and iterate without jeopardizing your business.

The Real Cost of High MOQ for Startups

Risk Factor500 pcs Order50 pcs Order
Upfront Investment$3,000-8,000$400-900
Storage Cost$100-300/month$0-20/month
Unsold Inventory RiskHighLow
Design IterationExpensiveAffordable
Cash Flow ImpactSevereMinimal

FABRIOZA's Startup Manufacturing Process

Step 1: Free Design Consultation

Send us your sketch, mood board, or reference garment. Our design team reviews feasibility, recommends fabrics and construction methods, and provides a cost estimate — all before you spend a dollar.

Step 2: Free Digital Mockup

Within 24 hours, we create a professional digital mockup showing your design on a garment template. See your concept come to life before committing to sampling.

Step 3: Low-Cost Physical Sample

Your first physical sample costs just $50-80. Sample cost and timeline depend on your product — share your details and a specialist will send an exact quote within 24 hours. See, feel, and wear your design before production.

Step 4: Small-Batch Production

Order 50 pieces to test the market. Run a pre-order campaign, seed influencers, or sell at a pop-up. Gather real customer feedback.

Step 5: Scale with Confidence

Once you've validated demand, scale to 100, 250, 500, or 1,000+ pieces. Our pricing gets better as you grow — but we never penalize you for starting small.

Cost Breakdown for Startup First Order

Here's what a typical startup's first order looks like:

Item50 pcs100 pcs250 pcs
Custom T-Shirt (180gsm)$8/unit$6.50/unit$5.50/unit
Custom Hoodie (320gsm)$18/unit$15/unit$12/unit
Sample Cost$60$60$60
Shipping (to USA)$80-120$120-180$200-300
Total Investment$580-720$790-1,040$1,635-2,310

Tips for Clothing Startup Success

  • Start with one hero product. Master one garment before expanding.
  • Use pre-orders. Sell before you manufacture to eliminate inventory risk.
  • Invest in photography. Great product photos increase conversion by 3-5x.
  • Build an email list. Own your customer relationships, don't rely on social algorithms.
  • Focus on fit. A great-fitting basic will outsell a poorly-fitting premium design.

Start Your Clothing Brand Today

50 pieces. $50 sample. Free mockup in 24 hours. Your brand starts here.
